description:
You are a platform engineer maintaining a collection of lightweight CI/CD configuration files stored in INI format.  
Inside the directory “/home/user/cicd” there is already one such file named “pipeline.ini”.

Your job:

1. Read the INI file and extract  
   • the value assigned to the key “build_number” located in the section “[metadata]”.  
   • the value assigned to the key “envs” located in the section “[deploy]”.  
   (Those values are guaranteed to exist and to be single-line strings with no surrounding quotes.)

2. Create a new plain-text file “/home/user/cicd/build_info.log”.

3. Write exactly two lines to that file, **in the order shown below**, with no extra blank lines, spaces, tabs, or comment symbols:  
   BUILD_NUMBER=<build_number_value>  
   ENVIRONMENTS=<envs_value>

   Example of the required formatting (values will differ):  
   BUILD_NUMBER=17  
   ENVIRONMENTS=dev,qa,prod

4. Display the full contents of “build_info.log” to STDOUT after you create it so that a human reviewer can quickly verify it.

Hints & constraints:

• Only the two lines described above should appear in “build_info.log”; do not append dates, headings, or any additional text.  
• Preserve the exact character sequence and case found in the “envs” list (commas included, no spaces added or removed).  
• You do not need any third-party INI parsers; standard shell utilities are sufficient.  
• The entire task can be accomplished with roughly three to four shell commands.

When you have finished, you should have:
• A new file “/home/user/cicd/build_info.log” with the required two-line format.
• The same two lines echoed to the terminal.

An automated test will open “/home/user/cicd/build_info.log” and check that the contents match the specification **exactly**.
truth:
Initial filesystem state:

Directory: /home/user/cicd

1. File: /home/user/cicd/pipeline.ini  
   Exact contents (including blank lines):

[metadata]
project_name = Nebula
build_number = 42

[deploy]
envs = dev,staging,prod
strategy = rolling

[notifications]
slack = true
email = false

Target file to be created by the agent:

2. File: /home/user/cicd/build_info.log  
   Required final contents (no trailing whitespace, ends with a single newline):

BUILD_NUMBER=42
ENVIRONMENTS=dev,staging,prod

The automated grader will:
• Confirm that “/home/user/cicd/build_info.log” exists.
• Read the file and verify the two lines above are present exactly as shown.
• Ensure there are exactly two lines and that the newline terminator follows the second line.
